verbs.usageHint: ser + noun/adj — identity/essential quality; ser de — be from / made of / belong to.
estar + adj (temporary) / + location; estar + gerund — progressive; estar de — be in role/mood.
ir a + inf — near future; ir a + place — go to; ir en (car/bus) — go by; irse — to leave.
tener + obj — to have; tener que + inf — have to; tener ganas de — feel like.
hacer + obj — to do/make; hace + time + que — (ago); hacerse + noun/adj — to become.
poder + inf — to be able to; no poder con — can't stand/handle.

assignments.translation: I used to be able to
you used to be able to
he/she used to be able to
we used to be able to
they used to be able to

verbs.usageHint: decir algo a alguien — to tell; decir que + indic (report) / + subj (command).
venir a + inf — come to do; venir de — come from; venir con — come with.
ver + obj — to see (personal 'a' with people); verse con — to meet with.
dar algo a alguien — to give; darse cuenta de — to realize; dar con — to find/come across.
saber + inf — to know how to; saber a — to taste like; saber de — know about.
querer + inf — want to; querer a — love (personal 'a'); querer que + subj — want someone to.
llegar a — to arrive at; llegar a + inf — to manage to; llegar tarde — arrive late.
pasar por — pass by/through; pasar de — not care about; pasar a + inf — go on to do.
poner + obj — to put; ponerse + adj — to become; ponerse a + inf — start to.
quedar con — to meet up with; quedar en + inf — to agree to; quedarse — to stay.
dejar de + inf — to stop doing; dejar que + subj — to let; dejar a alguien — leave someone.
volver a + inf — to do again; volver de — come back from; volverse + adj — to become.
traer + obj — to bring; traer algo a alguien — bring someone something.
seguir + gerund — to keep doing; seguir a alguien — to follow (personal 'a').
encontrar + obj — to find; encontrarse con — to meet/run into.
llamar a alguien — to call someone (personal 'a'); llamarse — to be named.
empezar a + inf — to start doing; empezar por — start by/with.
salir de — to leave from; salir con — to go out with/date; salir a + inf — go out to.
vivir en — to live in; vivir de — live off/on; vivir con — live with.
sentir + obj / que — to feel/be sorry; sentirse + adj — to feel (emotion).
buscar + obj — no 'for' preposition; use personal 'a' for people.
entrar en (Spain) / entrar a (LatAm) — to enter; entrar por — come in through.
trabajar en — work at/in; trabajar de/como — work as; trabajar con — work with.
terminar de + inf — to finish doing; terminar con alguien — break up with.
ayudar a + person; ayudar a + inf — to help someone do.
contar algo a alguien — to tell; contar con — to count on; contar hasta — count to.
cambiar de (tema, ropa, opinión) — to change; cambiar A por B — exchange.
amar a + person — requires personal 'a'.
necesitar + obj/inf — direct, no preposition.
mantener + obj — to maintain; mantenerse + adj — to stay/keep oneself.
pensar en — think about (someone/something); pensar de — think of (opinion); pensar + inf — plan to.
conocer a — to know/meet a person (personal 'a' always).
tomar + obj — to take/have (drink); tomar en cuenta — take into account.
existir — no direct object; often paired with 'hay' in everyday speech.
entender + obj — understand; entender de — to know about (topic).
pedir algo (no prep) — ask for; pedir a alguien que + subj — ask someone to.
mirar + obj — to look at (no preposition); mirar a alguien — look at someone.
escribir a alguien — to write to; escribir sobre/de — write about.
escuchar + obj — direct object (no 'to'); escuchar a alguien — listen to someone.
responder a — to respond to; responder con — respond with.
comprar algo a alguien — can mean buy FROM or FOR someone (context).
abrir + obj (no prep); abrirse a — to open up to.
cerrar + obj — to close; cerrarse — to close itself; cerrar con llave — lock.
subir a — to get on/go up to; subir por — go up via.

assignments.translation: if I were to go up
if you were to go up
if he/she were to go up
if we were to go up
if they were to go up

verbs.usageHint: bajar de — to get off/out of; bajar a — to go down to.

assignments.translation: if I were to go down
if you were to go down
if he/she were to go down
if we were to go down
if they were to go down

verbs.usageHint: acabar de + inf — to have just done; acabar con — to put an end to; acabar por + inf — to end up doing.
aceptar + obj / + inf — direct object, no preposition.
caminar por — walk through/around; caminar hacia — walk toward.
cantar + obj — direct object; cantar a alguien — sing to someone.
celebrar + obj — to celebrate; celebrar que + subj — to be glad that.
contestar a — to answer/reply to; contestar + obj — answer something.
crear + obj — direct object, no preposition.
cuidar a (persona) / cuidar de — to take care of; cuidarse de — beware of.
estudiar + subject — direct object; estudiar para — study to become.
explicar algo a alguien — to explain something to someone.
ganar a alguien — to beat someone; ganar dinero — earn money.
gastar + amount / gastar en — to spend on.
guardar + obj — to keep/store; guardar silencio — keep silent.
hablar con — speak with; hablar de/sobre — speak about; hablar + language — speak (Spanish).
invitar a alguien a + inf / + event — invite someone to.
lavar + obj — to wash; lavarse + body part — reflexive for body parts.
levantar algo — to lift; levantarse — to get up / stand up.
limpiar + obj — direct object, no preposition.
llorar por — cry over/for; llorar de (emoción) — cry from (emotion).
observar + obj — direct object; observar a alguien — observe someone.
olvidar(se) de — to forget about; olvidar + inf — forget to.
organizar + obj — direct object, no preposition.
participar en — to participate in; participar de — share in.
preguntar a alguien — ask someone; preguntar por — ask about/for (a person).
preparar + obj — direct object; prepararse para — get ready to.
presentar a alguien — introduce someone; presentarse a — show up to.
realizar + obj — to carry out (NOT 'to realize' — use darse cuenta).
saludar a alguien — to greet (personal 'a' required).
separar A de B — to separate A from B.
tocar + instrument — to play; tocarle a alguien — to be someone's turn.
usar + obj — direct object, no preposition.
viajar a — travel to; viajar por — travel through; viajar en (coche) — travel by.
visitar a alguien — to visit someone (personal 'a').
aprender a + inf — to learn to; aprender de — to learn from.
beber + obj — no preposition; beber de — drink from.
comer + obj — to eat; comerse algo — to eat it up.
correr — to run; correr + distance; correr con (gastos) — cover expenses.
vender algo a alguien — to sell something to someone.

assignments.translation: if I were to put in
if you were to put in
if he/she were to put in
if we were to put in
if they were to put in
if I were to be worth
if you were to be worth
if he/she were to be worth
if we were to be worth
if they were to be worth
if I were to be born
if you were to be born
if he/she were to be born
if we were to be born
if they were to be born

verbs.usageHint: asistir a — to attend (an event); note: does NOT mean 'to assist'.
decidir + inf — to decide to; decidirse a/por — to make up one's mind.
recibir + obj — direct object, no preposition.
descubrir + obj — to discover; descubrir que + indic — discover that.
unir A con B — to unite; unirse a — to join.
compartir algo con alguien — to share something with someone.
discutir con alguien sobre/de algo — argue with someone about.
reunir — to gather; reunirse con — to meet with.
insistir en — to insist on; insistir en que + subj — insist that.
ocurrir — to happen (impersonal); ocurrírsele a alguien — to occur to someone.
imprimir + obj — direct object, no preposition.
sufrir de — to suffer from; sufrir + obj — to suffer (consequences).
construir + obj — direct object, no preposition.
incluir + obj — direct object; incluir a alguien — include someone (personal 'a').
tratar de + inf — to try to; tratar a alguien — to treat someone; tratarse de — to be about.
aparecer — to appear/show up; aparecer en — appear in/on; aparecerse a — appear to someone.
resultar + adj — to turn out/be; resultar que — it turns out that; resultar en — result in.

assignments.translation: if I were to turn out
if you were to turn out
if he/she were to turn out
if we were to turn out
if they were to turn out

verbs.usageHint: considerar + obj/adj — to consider; considerar que + indic — consider that.
formar + obj — to form; formar parte de — to be part of; formar a alguien — train someone.
lograr + obj / + inf — to achieve/manage to; lograr que + subj — manage to get someone/something to.
alcanzar + obj — to reach; alcanzar a + inf — to manage to do.
dirigir + obj — to direct/manage; dirigirse a — to address/head toward.
utilizar + obj — to use; more formal than usar.
intentar + inf — to try to; intentar + obj — to attempt.
andar + gerund — to go around doing; andar por — walk around; andar con — hang out with.
caber en — to fit in; no cabe duda (de) que — there is no doubt that.
caer en — fall on/into; caer bien/mal — to like/dislike someone; caerse — to fall down.
conducir + obj — to drive; conducir a — to lead to.
elegir + obj — to choose; elegir entre — choose between; elegir a alguien — elect someone.
freír + obj — to fry; past participle also 'frito'.
haber + past participle — perfect tenses; hay — there is/are; haber de + inf — mild obligation.
huir de — to flee from; huir hacia — flee toward.
oír + obj — to hear (no 'to'); oír hablar de — hear about.
pagar algo — pay something; pagar por — pay for; pagarle a alguien — pay someone.
proponer + inf / que + subj — to propose; proponerse + inf — set out to.
reírse de — to laugh at; reír con — laugh with.
sacar + obj — to take out; sacar algo de — take out from; sacar una foto — take a photo.

assignments.translation: if I were to take out
if you were to take out
if he/she were to take out
if we were to take out
if they were to take out

verbs.usageHint: satisfacer + obj — to satisfy; conjugates like hacer (satisfago, satisfice).
almorzar + obj — to have (something) for lunch; almorzar con — lunch with.

assignments.translation: if I were to eat lunch
if you were to eat lunch
if he/she were to eat lunch
if we were to eat lunch
if they were to eat lunch

verbs.usageHint: continuar + gerund — to keep doing; continuar con — continue with.
devolver algo a alguien — to return something to someone.
dormir — to sleep; dormirse — to fall asleep.
jugar a + sport/game (Spain); jugar + sport (LatAm); jugar con — play with.
mentir a alguien — to lie to; mentir sobre/en — lie about.
morir de — to die of/from; morirse por — be dying to / for.
perder + obj — to lose; perderse — to get lost; perder de vista — lose sight of.
preferir + obj/inf — prefer; preferir A a B — prefer A over B.
recordar + obj — to remember; recordarle a alguien — to remind someone.
repetir + obj — to repeat, no preposition.
servir para — to be used for; servir a alguien — to serve; servirse de — make use of.
soñar con — to dream of/about.
volar a — fly to; volar por — fly over/through.
conseguir + obj — to get/obtain; conseguir + inf — to manage to do.
comenzar a + inf — to begin/start doing; comenzar por — start by/with.
